title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy eyes
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy eyes are infusions made from dried herbs that can help alleviate the discomfort and irritation caused by itchy eyes. These teas contain anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness. Chamomile and calendula are two popular herbs used in herbal teas for itchy eyes. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating eye irritations caused by allergies or infections. Calendula, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antifungal properties that can help soothe and heal irritated eyes. By using these herbal teas, individuals can experience relief from itchy eyes, leading to improved sleep, increased productivity, and overall better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tea helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes. The herb, which is rich in bioflavonoids and tannins, can help soothe inflamed tissue and tighten membranes, providing relief from itchiness. Additionally, eyebright tea has antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from environmental stressors and infections, further contributing to its ability to alleviate itchy eyes. Drinking eyebright tea or using it as a compress can provide a natural and effective solution for individuals experiencing itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tea helps reduce itchiness in eyes by decreasing inflammation, due to the presence of active constituents like tannins, phenolic acids, and flavonoids.
Astringent: The astringent properties of eyebright tea help alleviate itchy eyes by tightening and constricting the eye tissues, thanks to the astringent compounds like tannins.

Antihistamine: Eyebright tea contains antihistamine properties that help soothe itchy eyes by reducing the body's histamine response, making it an effective natural remedy for allergic conjunctivitis.
Antimicrobial: Eyebright tea inhibits the growth of bacteria that may cause eye itchiness, due to the presence of active constituents such as phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of eyebright tea help relieve pain and discomfort associated with itchy eyes, thanks to the presence of active compounds like alkaloids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in therapeutic compounds, the flowers of eyebright tea plant are used for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, providing relief for itchy eyes.
Leaves: High in soothing agents, the leaves of the eyebright tea plant help alleviate irritation and reduce inflammation, making them ideal for treating itchy eyes.
Seeds: Packed with beneficial compounds, the seeds of the eyebright tea plant offer a potent source of relief for itchy eyes, thanks to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried eyebright herb with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea to remove the solid herb particles.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink the tea or use it as a compress on itchy eyes for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe irritation and reduce redness. The tea is made from the leaves of the plantain plant, which contain compounds called flavonoids and tannins that have been shown to alleviate itching and inflammation. Additionally, plantain tea has natural antihistamine properties, which can help to relieve allergic reactions that may be causing the itchy eyes. Drinking plantain tea or using it as a compress can provide quick and effective relief for itchy eyes, making it a popular natural remedy for eye irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for itchy eyes as itchiness is often caused by inflammation in the eye area, and the active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antihistamine: Plantain tea acts as a natural antihistamine, which is crucial for itchy eyes since histamine release can cause itching, and the active constituents responsible for this property include mucilage, tannins, and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Plantain tea has antimicrobial properties, which are essential for itchy eyes as they can help prevent eye infections, and the active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, saponins, and essential oils.

Astringent: Plantain tea has astringent properties that help tighten and soothe the skin, which is important for itchy eyes as it can help reduce redness and irritation, and the active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and mucilage.

Demulcent: Plantain tea has demulcent properties that help soothe and protect mucous membranes, which is crucial for itchy eyes as it can help reduce inflammation and irritation, and the active constituents responsible for this property include mucilage and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the plantain tea plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the plantain tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have been known to provide relief from itchy eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the plantain tea plant contain mucilage, which can help to soothe and moisturize irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two large plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a clean, pesticide-free area.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly, then tear them into small pieces and place them in a pot.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the plantain leaves, then cover and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the plant material, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Use a clean dropper to apply the cooled plantain tea directly to the itchy eyes, blinking several times to distribute it evenly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes. The tea contains active compounds such as bisabolol and chamazulene, which have been shown to help soothe irritated skin and mucous membranes. When applied as a compress or gently rinsed over the eyes, chamomile tea can help alleviate itchiness, redness, and puffiness. Additionally, its calming effects may help relax the eyes and provide further relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains the active constituent azulene, which helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
Antihistamine: Chamomile tea contains antihistamine properties that can help alleviate symptoms of itchy eyes caused by allergies.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of German chamomile tea, due to its chamazulene content, can help prevent eye infections that may contribute to itchiness.
Astringent: The astringent properties of chamomile tea help to tighten and soothe the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and itchiness.
Analgesic: German chamomile tea contains analgesic compounds, such as bisabolol, that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them ideal for soothing itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids that have antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate irritation in the eyes.
Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a source of essential oils, which have calming and anti-inflammatory effects on itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 heaping teaspoon of dried German chamomile flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea to remove the chamomile flowers, leaving only the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Use a clean eye dropper to apply the cooled tea to itchy eyes, being careful to avoid touching the dropper to the eye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe irritation and reduce inflammation in the eyes. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids that have been shown to possess healing and anti-inflammatory effects. When used as a compress or eyewash, pot marigold tea can help alleviate itchiness, redness, and swelling in the eyes, providing quick and natural relief from eye discomfort. Its soothing and calming effects make it an ideal remedy for individuals who experience itchy eyes due to allergies, dryness, or environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce itchy eyes by decreasing inflammation, thanks to active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antihistamine: The tea can alleviate itchiness by blocking histamine, a compound that contributes to allergic reactions, due to its flavonoid content.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tea inhibits bacterial growth, reducing the risk of secondary infections that can exacerbate itchy eyes, due to its antimicrobial compounds such as essential oils and tannins.
Astringent: The tea can help soothe itchy eyes by causing tissue contraction, thereby providing temporary relief, due to its tannin content.
Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can worsen itchiness, by containing potent antioxidants like flavonoids and carotenoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in anti-inflammatory flavonoids, making them ideal for soothing itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain therapeutic compounds, which can help alleviate eye irritation when used in medicinal tea.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of anti-inflammatory agents and antioxidants, providing relief for itchy eyes when brewed in a tea.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of boiling water over 1 teaspoon of dried pot marigold flowers.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10 minutes to extract the herb's beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the spent flowers and retain the liquid.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature for your eyes.
Use a clean eyedropper to apply the cooled tea to your itchy eyes, blinking gently to distribute it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can soothe irritation and reduce redness. The tea is made from the leaves of the comfrey plant, which is rich in allantoin, a substance known for its healing and anti-inflammatory effects. When used as a compress, comfrey tea can help to alleviate itchiness and discomfort caused by allergies, dryness, or infection. Its natural anti-histamine properties can also help to relieve symptoms of eye allergies, providing quick and effective relief for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea contains allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which help reduce inflammation, providing relief for itchy eyes caused by irritation or inflammation.
Astringent: Comfrey tea contains tannins that have an astringent effect, helping to tighten and soothe irritated skin around the eyes, reducing itchiness.
Demulcent: Comfrey tea contains mucilage, which forms a soothing film over the eyes, helping to alleviate itchiness and protect the eyes from further irritation.
Analgesic: Comfrey tea contains compounds like symphytum officinale, which have analgesic properties, helping to relieve pain and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
Antimicrobial: Comfrey tea has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids, which can help prevent eye infections that may cause itchiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy eyes.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes.
Rhyzomes: Packed with allantoin, which has been shown to have anti-irritant and anti-inflammatory effects, beneficial for itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried comfrey leaves, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
After simmering, remove the pot from heat and let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea to remove the comfrey leaves, then transfer the liquid to a clean container.
Use a clean dropper to apply the cooled comfrey tea to itchy eyes, being careful to avoid touching the dropper to the eye area.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's Wort tea helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can alleviate eye irritation. This herbal tea is rich in bioflavonoids and hypericin, which are known to reduce redness, swelling, and itching in the eyes. Moreover, St. John's Wort tea can help soothe dry eyes by increasing tear production, providing much-needed relief from itchiness. Its calming effects on the eyes make it an excellent natural remedy for people who suffer from seasonal allergies or environmental factors that cause eye itchiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye redness and irritation, important for itchy eyes, due to its active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.
Antihistamine: It contains antihistamine properties that can alleviate symptoms of allergies, such as itchy eyes, because of its active compounds like hypericin and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent eye infections, important for itchy eyes, due to its active constituents like hyperforin and hypericin.
Analgesic: It has pain-relieving properties that can help soothe eye discomfort, important for itchy eyes, due to its active constituents like hypericin and essential oils.
Astringent: St. John's wort tea has astringent properties that can help reduce eye secretions, important for itchy eyes, due to its active constituents like tannins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St. John's Wort contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of St. John's Wort contain similar compounds to the flowers, and can also help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes.
Buds: The buds of St. John's Wort are rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the eyes from damage and reduce inflammation, making them useful for soothing itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried St. John's Wort herb with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the tea to steep for 10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the herb particles, preserving only the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature, suitable for eye rinsing.
Use a clean eyecup or a sterile shot glass to rinse each itchy eye with the St. John's Wort tea for soothing relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can alleviate inflammation and irritation in the eyes. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the yarrow plant, which are rich in active compounds such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins. These compounds work together to soothe the eyes, reduce redness, and alleviate itchiness caused by allergies, dryness, or environmental factors. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a compress can provide quick relief and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent properties help tighten and soothe irritated skin around the eyes, reducing redness and itchiness. This is attributed to the tannins present in yarrow.
Antihistamine: Yarrow tea contains antihistamine compounds that help alleviate allergic reactions, which can cause itchy eyes. The main antihistamine compound in yarrow is isoquercitrin.
Analgesic: Yarrow tea's analgesic properties help relieve pain and discomfort caused by itchy eyes. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like azulene and camphor.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties help protect the eyes from potential infections, promoting overall eye health. This is attributed to the presence of lactones and essential oils in yarrow.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and flavonoids, yarrow flowers help alleviate itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and irritation.
Leaves: Containing tannins and volatile oils, yarrow leaves can soothe itchy eyes by providing astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids and antioxidants, yarrow seeds can help alleviate itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for 2-3 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers or leaves to a tea infuser.
Pour the hot water over the yarrow herb in the infuser.
Steep the herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the infuser and enjoy the warm yarrow tea, avoiding contact with itchy eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can soothe irritation and reduce redness. Horsetail is rich in silica, a mineral that can strengthen the tissues around the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, horsetail tea has antioxidant and anti-microbial properties that can help protect the eyes from environmental stressors and infections, further reducing the likelihood of itchy eyes. Drinking horsetail tea or using it as a compress can provide quick and effective relief from itchy eyes, making it a popular natural remedy for eye irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea reduces inflammation, which is important for itchy eyes as inflammation can exacerbate the itching sensation. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Astringent: Horsetail tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and dry up secretions, reducing eye discharge and alleviating itchiness. Tannins present in horsetail give it this astringent quality.

Antimicrobial: Horsetail tea can help prevent eye infections, which can cause or worsen itchy eyes. The antimicrobial properties come from compounds like silica, calcium, and other minerals found in horsetail.

Antihistamine: Horsetail tea can help alleviate allergic reactions, which can cause itchy eyes. The active constituents responsible for this property include various flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can inhibit histamine release.

Diuretic: Horsetail tea promotes fluid elimination, which can help reduce eye puffiness and alleviate itchiness. The diuretic effect is primarily due to the high silica and potassium content in horsetail.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the horsetail tea plant are rich in silica and antioxidants, making them effective in reducing inflammation and itchiness in eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of the horsetail tea plant contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making them useful for soothing itchy eyes.
Stems: The stems of the horsetail tea plant contain high levels of silica and other minerals, which can help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the horsetail tea plant have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them helpful in reducing itchiness and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the horsetail herb.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before using it as a compress for itchy eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blackcurrant tea helps with itchy eyes because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate eye irritation. The anthocyanins in blackcurrant are known to reduce inflammation, which can help soothe itchy eyes. Additionally, blackcurrant tea is rich in vitamin C, which can boost the immune system and help fight off any potential eye irritants. The tea's warmth can also provide a soothing effect, helping to relieve any discomfort caused by itchy eyes. Furthermore, blackcurrant tea is caffeine-free, making it a great alternative to other caffeinated drinks that can dehydrate the body and worsen eye itchiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Blackcurrant tea contains anthocyanins that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce eye redness and irritation caused by itchy eyes.
Antihistamine: The presence of quercetin in blackcurrant tea provides antihistamine effects, alleviating symptoms of allergic conjunctivitis, a common cause of itchy eyes.
Antioxidant: Blackcurrants are rich in vitamin C and other antioxidants, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and support overall eye health, reducing the likelihood of itchy eyes.
Immune-boosting: The high vitamin C content in blackcurrant tea strengthens the immune system, helping the body fight off infections and allergens that can lead to itchy eyes.
Astringent: Tannins found in blackcurrant tea have astringent properties, which can help soothe and tighten eye tissues, reducing redness and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the blackcurrant tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for soothing itchy eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the blackcurrant tea plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and may help alleviate itchy eyes.
Berries: The berries of the blackcurrant tea plant are high in vitamin C and other antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe itchy eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the blackcurrant tea plant are a good source of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which has anti-inflammatory properties and may help alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of fresh blackcurrant leaves and 2 cups of water, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow the water to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Add honey to taste, stir well until fully dissolved.
Drink 2-3 cups of this herbal tea daily to soothe itchy eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tea helps with itchy eyes because it contains flavonoids and other antioxidant compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness. Additionally, maidenhair tree tea has been shown to have antihistamine effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergies that may be causing itchy eyes. The tea can be consumed or used as a compress to soothe the eyes directly. However, it is important to note that while maidenhair tree tea may help with itchy eyes, it is not a substitute for medical treatment, especially in cases of severe or persistent itchiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair tree tea contains active constituents like ginkgolides and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation, which is important for itchy eyes as inflammation can exacerbate the itchiness and discomfort.

Antihistamine: Maidenhair tree tea has antihistamine properties due to the presence of ginkgolides, which can help alleviate itchy eyes by blocking the action of histamine, a compound that contributes to allergic reactions.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in maidenhair tree tea, provided by flavonoids and terpenoids, helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, reducing itchiness and promoting overall eye health.
Immunomodulatory: Maidenhair tree tea has immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response, reducing the likelihood of allergic reactions that lead to itchy eyes.

Anti-microbial: Maidenhair tree tea exhibits anti-microbial properties, which can help prevent eye infections that may cause or worsen itchy eyes. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginkgolides, bilobalide, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the maidenhair tree tea plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe eye irritation.
Flowers: The flowers of the maidenhair tree tea plant are used in medicinal teas for itchy eyes because they contain antioxidants that can help reduce redness and inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of the maidenhair tree tea plant are used in medicinal teas for itchy eyes due to their high levels of vitamin E, which can help moisturize and soothe dry, itchy eyes.
Bark: The bark of the maidenhair tree tea plant is used in medicinal teas for itchy eyes due to its astringent properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 maidenhair tree (Ginkgo biloba) leaves and rinse them thoroughly under running water.
Bring 2 cups of water to a boil, then add the leaves and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Add honey to taste, if desired, for added sweetness and potential anti-inflammatory properties.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea daily to help soothe and relieve itchy eyes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy eyes with herbal teas is by using chamomile or fennel tea bags. Steep the tea bags in hot water for several minutes, then let them cool down. Once they're at a comfortable temperature, place the tea bags on closed eyelids for 10-15 minutes. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day. Both chamomile and fennel have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated eyes and reduce itchiness. This natural remedy is a great alternative to over-the-counter medications and can provide instant relief.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal teas include dry eye, eye strain, and pink eye. Chamomile tea is a popular choice for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in soothing irritated eyes and reducing inflammation. Meanwhile, calendula tea is known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a suitable treatment for pink eye and other eye infections. For dry eye, fennel tea can help stimulate tear production and reduce inflammation. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment for eye ailments.
